title: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and ConvertKit. Find the best email marketing platform for your needs, pricing, features, and customer ratings.
h1: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit: Detailed Comparison for Email Marketing
intro: Mailchimp and ConvertKit are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Mailchimp positions itself as an all-in-one marketing suite for beginners and small businesses, while ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators, bloggers, and online course sellers. Choosing between them depends on your business type, budget, and specific feature requirements.
choose_a_if: You're a beginner looking for an affordable, comprehensive marketing platform with a low barrier to entry
You need the lowest possible starting price and want to maximize free tier features
You prefer a well-established platform with over 20 years of market experience
choose_b_if: You're a creator, blogger, or online course seller who needs commerce and sequence automation
You want a higher free tier limit (1,000 vs 500 subscribers) to grow before paying
You prioritize customer satisfaction and community-focused features

faq.a: Neither is objectively "better" — it depends on your needs. Mailchimp excels for budget-conscious beginners and small businesses with its lower price point and broader marketing tools. ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators and offers better customer ratings, more generous free tier limits, and specialized features like commerce and visual automations.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or ConvertKit?
faq.a: Mailchimp is significantly cheaper, starting at $13/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Mailchimp also offers a more generous free tier with 500 free subscribers and no feature limitations, making it the budget-friendly choice for cost-conscious users.

faq.q: Can I switch from ConvertKit to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support subscriber list exports, allowing you to migrate your contacts between services. However, you'll lose any ConvertKit-specific automations and sequences during the transfer, so plan your migration carefully and test your new workflows in Mailchimp before switching completely.
